meta_title: For Founders: debugging with AI to Solve no portfolio (Beginner Tutorial)
meta_description: Learn how to debugging with AI and overcome no portfolio. A Beginner-level Tutorial using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Founders: Debugging with AI to Solve No Portfolio
content.seo_description: Learn how to leverage AI debugging to overcome a lack of portfolio. A founder-focused guide using Antigravity and Claude Code to build shipping velocity.
content.answer_block: Overcome the 'no portfolio' trap by using AI to debug and ship real, revenue-generating SaaS features rapidly. By integrating Antigravity for architecture and Claude Code for iterative execution, you transform complex bugs into completed features, building a credible, live-code portfolio that proves your technical competence to potential investors and paying clients.
content.intro:
You’re staring at a blank repository, the silence of a missing portfolio screaming louder than the competition’s marketing budget. In the high-stakes world of SaaS, potential customers don't care about your resume; they care about what you can ship. The 'no portfolio' problem isn't a lack of talent—it's a bottleneck in execution speed.

Today, we break that cycle. You don't need years of legacy experience to build a professional-grade portfolio. You need to weaponize AI to debug, refactor, and deploy at a velocity that makes traditional developers look like they are standing still. We are turning your current development hurdles into the very proof of work that will close your next big deal.

steps.title: Define the High-Value MVP Feature
steps.description: Identify a small, complex feature that solves a real pain point. Don't build a to-do list; build a payment gateway integration or an automated data parser.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Act as a CTO. Define a modular, scalable architecture for a SaaS feature that handles user authentication and Stripe subscription logic. Provide the folder structure and core dependencies.

steps.title: Automated Debugging of Boilerplate
steps.description: Use AI to scan your initial setup for common configuration errors that usually stall founders for days.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Analyze my current directory for potential misconfigurations in the environment setup. Identify security risks or missing type definitions.

steps.title: Iterative Feature Implementation
steps.description: Implement the core logic using Claude Code to handle the heavy lifting of writing standard API controllers.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write a robust Express controller for handling webhook events from Stripe, including error handling and logging for production observability.

steps.title: AI-Driven Test Coverage
steps.description: A portfolio without tests is just a collection of fragile scripts. Use AI to generate unit tests that prove your code is production-ready.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate Jest test suites for the Stripe handler, covering edge cases like invalid signatures and API timeouts.

steps.title: Final Deployment and Documentation
steps.description: Document your process to show your 'thought process'—this is more valuable to recruiters/investors than the code itself.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a professional README.md that explains the architecture choices, the technical challenges faced, and how the AI was used to solve them.

pro_tips: Treat your Git commit history as a diary; use AI to write meaningful, professional commit messages that tell a story of problem-solving.
Always ask your AI tool to 'explain the trade-offs' of a specific architectural choice; this makes you look like a senior architect in interviews.
Focus on 'shipping' over 'perfecting'. A live URL is worth 10,000 lines of invisible, un-deployed local code.
Use Claude Code to refactor your code into smaller, single-responsibility modules to make it easier to explain during deep-dive demos.
Automate your feedback loop by integrating AI error reporting; showing you have an observability strategy is a massive green flag for stakeholders.
content.next_steps: Once your first feature is live, push it to GitHub, write a concise case study on your site, and start reaching out to your first 10 potential users to validate the product-market fit.
content.conversion_block: You now have the technical blueprint to stop dreaming and start shipping. Every line of code you generate is an asset that builds your professional reputation. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
